creator: freebsd7-pr@i-cnct.com
text:
read /usr/src/updating
  read all current pr reports
  previous OS FreeBSD 6.3
  make.conf has no build options (although have also built with NO_PROFILE=true and CFLAGS= -0
-pipe but no difference in results)

  building from src

#  1.  `cd /usr/src'       (or to the directory containing your source tree).
#  2.  `make buildworld'
#  3.  `make buildkernel KERNCONF=YOUR_KERNEL_HERE'     (default is GENERIC).
#  4.  `make installkernel KERNCONF=YOUR_KERNEL_HERE'   (default is GENERIC).
#       [steps 3. & 4. can be combined by using the "kernel" target]
#  5.  `reboot'        (in single user mode: boot -s from the loader prompt).
#  6.  `mergemaster -p'
#  7.  `make installworld'
#  8.  `make delete-old'
#  9.  `mergemaster'
# 10.  `reboot'

After final mergemaster and reboot, /boot/loader gives error:

Guessed BIOS device 0x%x not found by probes, defaulting to disk0:
Can't work out which disk we are booting from.

[WORKAROUND]

Interrupt the boot process before the error, boot from:

boot: /boot/loader.old

or use the fix-it disk and copy loader to loader.sav, loader.old to loader

# fsck_ffs /dev/da0s1
# mkdir /mnt/root
# mount -rw /dev/da0s1a /mnt/root
# mv /mnt/root/boot/loader /mnt/root/boot/loader.sav
# cp /mnt/root/boot/loader.old /mnt/root/boot/loader

I can then boot from loader.old

# dmesg

Fix: 

[WORKAROUND]

Break out of the boot process before the failure and:

boot: /boot/loader.old

Then
# mv /boot/loader /boot/loader.sav
# cp /boot/loader.old /boot/loader
How-To-Repeat: Install FreeBSD6.3 and upgrade to FreeBSD7 using src.

After final mergemaster, reboot.

Error happens in trying to load from /boot/loader

Have other server/system that is similar in hardware but only 1 processor.  Build works on this machine (although I think that is a red herring). Have seen some other similar problem reports

http://groups.google.com/group/lucky.freebsd.questions/browse_thread/thread/f18554c471e8d29e/bbb6c94e7b6e655d?lnk=raot
